name: Puig de l'Àliga and Els Tres Turons Loop
name_original: Gorg de la Mola, Les Rovires – Ruta Circular
description: Hike the moderate 5.3-mile Gorg de la Mola, Les Rovires – Circular Route to discover waterfalls, natural pools, and lush Mediterranean scene
region_intro: Hiking around Sant Andreu De La Barca provides access to a varied natural landscape, situated on the right bank of the Llobregat River and enveloped by the Ordal massif. The municipality features significant natural areas, with forests covering a third of its surface. This topography includes a valley surrounded by small hills, wooded areas, and prominent peaks like Pic de l'Àliga. The region also features natural highlights such as the Gorg de la Mola with its waterfalls and natural pools. Best hiking trails around Sant Andreu De La Barca The most popular hiking route is Puig de l'Àliga and Els Tres Turons Loop, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 34 minutes to complete, offering views from the peak and across the three hills. Another top favourite among local hikers is Gorg de la Mola and Pic de l'Àliga Loop, a moderate 5.0 miles (8.1 km) path that explores waterfalls, natural pools, and ascends to Pic de l'Àliga. Local hikers also love the Camí de la Fàbrica (Can Palet) – View from the Pic de l'Àliga loop from Sant Andreu de la Barca, a 6.3 miles (10.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain to a viewpoint, often completed in about 2 hours 59 minutes. Hiking around Sant Andreu De La Barca is defined by the Ordal massif, the Llobregat River, and forested hills, featuring waterfalls and natural pools. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Sant Andreu De La Barca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 300 reviews. More than 1600 hikers have used komoot to explore Sant Andreu De La Barca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Sant Andreu De La Barca?
A: Sant Andreu De La Barca offers a diverse network of over 35 hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, with 10 easy, 23 moderate, and 2 difficult options to explore the region's natural beauty.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Sant Andreu De La Barca has 10 easy hiking trails. A great option for a shorter, family-friendly walk is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19100117" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg de la Mola – Cart Ruts loop from Sant Andreu de la Barca&lt;/a&gt;, which is just over 2.5 km and features natural pools.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls or natural pools in the area?
A: Absolutely. The Gorg de la Mola area is renowned for its impressive waterfalls and natural pools. You can explore these lush Mediterranean scenes on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1356503848/gorg-de-la-mola-and-pic-de-l-aliga-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg de la Mola and Pic de l'Àliga Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which combines waterfalls with a climb to a prominent peak.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or landmarks I can see on a hike?
A: The region offers several scenic viewpoints. The Pic de l'Àliga (or Puig de l'Àliga) is a prominent natural landmark, providing excellent vistas. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939064622/puig-de-l-aliga-and-els-tres-turons-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de l'Àliga and Els Tres Turons Loop&lt;/a&gt; will take you to this peak. Additionally, the Mirador del Cerro de la Torre dels Còdols offers panoramic views of the Llobregat River.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Sant Andreu De La Barca?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Sant Andreu De La Barca are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939064622/puig-de-l-aliga-and-els-tres-turons-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de l'Àliga and Els Tres Turons Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33861428" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camí de la Fàbrica (Can Palet) – View from the Pic de l'Àliga loop from Sant Andreu de la Barca&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Sant Andreu De La Barca?
A: The trails in Sant Andreu De La Barca are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 345 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the natural beauty of the Ordal massif, and the presence of waterfalls and natural pools.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the area around Sant Andreu De La Barca features several historical sites and castles. You can explore places like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/951471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellciuró Ruins&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1066148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fossada Tower (Castellbisbal)&lt;/a&gt;. For more information, you can check the guide on &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/3822351/castles-around-sant-andreu-de-la-barca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castles around Sant Andreu De La Barca&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Sant Andreu De La Barca?
A: Sant Andreu De La Barca offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for exploring the forested areas and peaks. Summer can be warm, but early mornings or late afternoons are still suitable, especially on shaded trails.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: While most routes are moderate, there are a couple of more difficult trails for those seeking a challenge. For a longer moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5322944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de les Deveses – Bench at Turó de Can Xandri loop from el Palau&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 13 km with significant elevation changes.

Q: What kind of natural features define the hiking experience in Sant Andreu De La Barca?
A: The hiking experience is defined by the Ordal massif, the Llobregat River, and extensive forested hills. You'll encounter varied topography, from riverine paths to elevated peaks, with highlights including the Gorg de la Mola's waterfalls and natural pools, and panoramic views from peaks like Pic de l'Àliga.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Llobregat River?
A: Yes, being situated on the right bank of the Llobregat River, Sant Andreu De La Barca offers access to fluvial paths. These trails are suitable for walking and cycling, allowing you to enjoy the riverside environment and its unique ecosystem.

The Hermano fountain is a public space in Sant Andreu de la Barca located near the Riera de Corbera, which was rehabilitated in 2014. This historic fountain, which is located below the Sant Andreu de la Barca School , was formerly used to clean clothes.[1]


Font de l'Hermano, in Sant Andreu de la Barca
There is evidence that the area popularly known as the source of Hermano was already in the 19th century a place frequented by holidaymakers and an ideal place to spend the afternoon and have a meal because of the existence of a source. Originally the land was owned by Cal Jansana and the Ca l'Hermano hí family had a leased orchard. These two facts are what end up giving the place its name.

Attributing words to families is very common and Ca l'Hermano owes its name to a former relative who had entered a convent to become a friar (hermano). And although he left the habits before he was ordained.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Gorg de la Mola, Les Rovires – Circular Route?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route, and while specific parking areas aren't detailed, it's generally accessible from Les Rovires. Hikers often look for parking near the start of the trailheads in the area. Given its proximity to Barcelona, many visitors drive to the starting point.

faqs.question: Is the Gorg de la Mola, Les Rovires – Circular Route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty with your canine companion. However, always ensure your dog is under control, especially in areas with other hikers or wildlife, and be mindful of any local regulations regarding leashes.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this route is typically during spring or autumn when the weather is milder and the vegetation is lush. Summer can be hot, and mosquitoes may be prevalent, especially near water sources. Winter offers a different charm but can have slippery sections due to water flow.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access the Gorg de la Mola, Les Rovires – Circular Route. It is a publicly accessible natural area.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. While generally accessible, some sections can be challenging. You'll encounter mostly accessible paths, but good fitness and sure-footedness are recommended. Parts near the river can be slippery due to water-washed rocks, and some areas may have been damaged by floods, requiring careful navigation or even holding onto cables. The final descent to the main waterfall is steep and rocky.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: The trail is considered moderate. The initial 1.7 km stretch towards the Gorg de la Mola is relatively easy and family-friendly. However, other sections demand more caution due to steep, rocky, or potentially slippery paths, making the full circular route more suitable for those with some hiking experience and appropriate footwear.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in natural beauty. Key highlights include the impressive Gorg de la Mola with its waterfalls and natural pools, the Rovires Spring, the Pond of the Carinyo Spring, Font de la Teula, and Droc Rock. You'll also follow the Riera de Corbera, offering views of various small waterfalls and lush Mediterranean vegetation.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, it's essential to wear appropriate mountain footwear with good grip. Bringing water, snacks, and sun protection is always advisable. If hiking in summer, consider insect repellent due to potential mosquitoes. A first-aid kit is also recommended for any minor scrapes or falls on rocky sections.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or insider tips for this route?
faqs.answer: Be aware that sections near the river can be very slippery due to smooth, water-washed rocks. Some paths may have been damaged by floods, requiring extra caution and potentially using cables for support. The final descent to the main waterfall is steep and rocky. For a more relaxed experience, consider hiking during off-peak hours or seasons to avoid crowds, as it's a popular spot near Barcelona.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Accés font de l'Hermano, Plaça Onze de Setembre, and Passatge dels Segadors, among others. While these are not major long-distance trails, they are part of the local network.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restrooms or cafes along the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail itself is a natural route, so there are no dedicated restrooms or cafes directly along the path. It's advisable to use facilities in nearby towns or at the trailhead before starting your hike and to carry out all your waste. Pack enough water and snacks for the duration of your trip.
